prerequisites: A Partnerize account with API access (sign in at https://console.partnerize.com).
Your User Application Key (the application_key config field), obtained from Partnerize Account Settings.
Your User API Key (the user_api_key config field), obtained from Partnerize Account Settings.

authMethods.name: HTTP Basic Authentication (Application Key + User API Key)
authMethods.summary: The Partnerize API uses HTTP Basic Authentication. Both the application_key (network-level key, used as the username) and the user_api_key (the key identifying the user on whose behalf requests are made, used as the password) are required to authenticate every API request. The connector exposes these as the secret fields application_key and user_api_key.

setupSteps.title: Sign in to the Partnerize console
setupSteps.body: Log in to your Partnerize account at https://console.partnerize.com and open Account Settings.

setupSteps.title: Retrieve your Application Key
setupSteps.body: In Account Settings, locate the User Application Key and copy it. This is the network-level application_key required by the connector.

setupSteps.title: Retrieve your User API Key
setupSteps.body: In Account Settings, locate the User API Key and copy it. This identifies the user on whose behalf API requests are made and is the user_api_key required by the connector.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the copied values into the connector's application_key and user_api_key secret fields. Both are required and are sent together as HTTP Basic Authentication credentials (application_key as username, user_api_key as password) on each request to the Partnerize API.

sources.title: Partnerize API Documentation - Introduction (Access and Authentication)
